channel.title: Frontion News - Breaking Geopolitics
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇽🇰 Kosovo court sentences three Serb separatists for 2023 monastery attack
item.description: Two men received life sentences and another a 30-year term for the Banjska monastery siege that killed one police officer and three gunmen.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
item.description: Israel's prime minister says he is in excellent physical condition after having a malignant tumor removed, raising questions about leadership stability during wartime.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU approves 106 billion euro loan package for Ukraine after Hungary lifts veto
item.description: The European Union approved the massive loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for two years, ending months of political deadlock.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese firms exploiting US AI models
item.description: The administration is targeting foreign tech companies exploitation of US artificial intelligence models, singling out China as the gap narrows in the AI race.

item.title: 🇨🇴🇻🇪 Colombian president visits Venezuela in first trip since Maduro abduction
item.description: Border security is expected to loom large as Colombia leader Gustavo Petro meets interim Venezuelan President Delcy Rodriguez.

item.title: 🇪🇺 NATO official: Trump cannot expel members over Iran stance
item.description: A NATO official confirmed that the alliance founding treaty has no provision for suspending or expelling members, responding to reports the Pentagon considered punishing Spain for refusing to support US-Iran operations. Spain rejected using its bases for Iran strikes and opposed the 5% defense spending proposal.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine completes 73rd prisoner swap, brings home 193 POWs
item.description: Ukraine and Russia exchanged 193 prisoners each in the continuation of the Easter exchange. Released soldiers include those held in Chechnya, wounded personnel, and some facing fabricated criminal charges. Over 7,000 Ukrainians have been returned since the war began.

item.title: 🤖 Ukraine to field 25,000 ground robots for frontline logistics
item.description: Ukraines military plans to deploy 25,000 ground robots to replace soldiers for frontline logistics operations. Ground robots ran more than 9,000 missions in March, triple Novembers count, and captured their first Russian position last summer.

item.title: ⚓ Three US carriers operate in Middle East for first time since 2003
item.description: Three US aircraft carriers are operating in Central Commands area of responsibility simultaneously for the first time since 2003, reflecting the massive naval presence deployed for the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🚢 Pentagon chief says US Iran blockade going global
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced the US blockade on Iran is expanding globally, with the military redirecting 34 vessels as part of the pressure campaign.

item.title: 🛩️ US deploys three aircraft carriers to Middle East
item.description: For the first time since 2003, the US has three aircraft carriers in the Middle East as tensions with Iran remain high amid a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: 🤝 Iran to make offer satisfying US demands, says Trump
item.description: Iran is preparing to make an offer aimed at satisfying US demands, according to Trump, as diplomatic efforts continue through Pakistan.

item.title: 🛡️ US Space Force awards $3.2B in missile defense contracts
item.description: The US Space Force has tapped 12 firms for the Golden Dome missile defense program, a major new defense initiative.

item.title: 🕊️ Trump envoys head to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks
item.description: US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are traveling to Pakistan as Iran signals willingness to make an offer satisfying US demands. Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi is already in Islamabad but no direct meeting with US delegation is confirmed.

item.title: ⚖️ US adds firing squads, electrocution to federal execution methods
item.description: The Trump administration is expanding federal execution methods to include firing squads, electrocution, and gassing as part of a broader push to prioritize the death penalty.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Israel kills at least 12 Palestinians in Gaza amid ceasefire
item.description: Hamas says the Israeli escalation represents the failure of the international community to uphold the Gaza truce, as ceasefire violations continue.

item.title: 🤖 Google to invest up to $40 billion in AI rival Anthropic
item.description: Google is planning a major investment in Anthropic as competition heats up in the AI arms race between tech giants.

item.title: 🇪🇺 NATO says no provision to expel members after US report on suspending Spain
item.description: NATO stated its founding treaty has no mechanism for suspending or expelling members after a leaked Pentagon email suggested the US could seek to suspend Spain over its lack of support for the Iran war. Spain has refused to allow US bases on its territory for attacks on Iran.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK reaffirms Falklands sovereignty after US Pentagon memo raises review
item.description: Downing Street stated Falklands sovereignty 'rests with the UK' after an internal Pentagon email reportedly suggested the US could reassess support for Britain's claim to the islands in retaliation for the UK not supporting the Iran war.

item.title: 🏛️ US Justice Department drops criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell
item.description: The Department of Justice has dropped its criminal investigation into Fed Chairman Jerome Powell, clearing the path for confirmation of his successor Kevin Warsh. Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU anti-fraud office opens formal investigation into Mandelson
item.description: The European Anti-Fraud Office (Olaf) has launched a formal investigation into former UK ambassador to the US Peter Mandelson over allegations of misconduct during his time as EU trade commissioner, including exchanges with Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: ⚠️ UN report: Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international humanitarian law
item.description: A UN human rights report found Israeli strikes on residential buildings in Lebanon may amount to serious violations of international law, killing over 2,400 people since March. Hezbollah rocket fire into Israel also likely violated IHL, the report said.

item.title: ⚖️ Trump administration to prioritize death penalty, expand use of firing squads
item.description: The Justice Department announced plans to expand federal death penalty implementation, including firing squads and lethal gas. The policy reverses Biden's moratorium and calls for expanding federal death row capacity.

item.title: 🇸🇾 Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre arrested in Syria
item.description: Amjad Youssef, wanted for the 2013 mass killing of civilians in Damascus, has been arrested after a security operation. Video evidence showed Syrian soldiers leading bound, blindfolded victims to a pit before shooting them.

item.title: 🚢 Pirates hijack oil tanker off Somalia with 17 crew amid Hormuz disruption
item.description: Pirates seized the oil tanker Honour 25 with 17 crew near the Somali coast. The hijacking highlights renewed piracy concerns as the Iran conflict disrupts Gulf shipping routes. The vessel was carrying 18,500 barrels of oil.
